articles.headline: This Underrated KFC Menu Item 'Doesn't Even Taste Like Fast Food'
articles.author: Julia Holland
articles.summary: KFC is one of the oldest fast food fried chicken chains and has established itself as a classic in the U.S. and beyond. While KFC keeps things fresh with new deals and menu items, it also has tried-and-true classics with a loyal fanbase. According to this fanbase, customers are sleeping on one of the most underrated KFC menu items: the chicken pot pie. KFC's chicken pot pie features a filling of peas, carrots, shredded chicken, and potatoes in a thick gravy sauce under a flaky crust. As per KFC regulars on Reddit, it "doesn't even taste like fast food." One fan even claimed that "there isn't a better single tasting item at a fast food mega chain" and that it's more akin to "something someone's grandma made." Multiple customers have echoed the sentiment. One Redditor described it as a "crust [that] is light and flaky in a really good way. The chicken and vegetables are balanced in a delicious gravy." Customers have also complimented the crust-to-filling ratio, breaking up the top crust to mix it into the filling for a well-rounded bite every time.

articles.headline: Authorities Investigate Taco Bell And Lettuce In Multistate Cyclosporiasis Outbreak
articles.author: Lena H. Sun
articles.summary: Federal and state health officials are investigating whether Taco Bell restaurants played a role in one of the largest U.S. outbreaks of a gastrointestinal illness caused by a parasite that contaminates fresh produce, according to two individuals familiar with the investigation. The two individuals spoke on the condition of anonymity because the investigation is ongoing into the outbreak of the infection that can cause days of diarrhea and has sickened thousands. Notices posted at some Detroit-area Taco Bell restaurants last week told customers the chain was “currently unable to sell Lettuce, Cilantro Onion, Pico de Gallo, and Guacamole due to a nationwide recall,” according to media reports. Authorities have not yet identified a specific produce grower or supplier responsible for the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis that has hospitalized more than 80 people. Public health officials say there could be multiple culprits.

articles.headline: Chipotle Backs Innovators Transforming Food Systems
articles.author: Brooke Just
articles.summary: Six young companies have landed backing from Chipotle Mexican Grill’s Cultivate Next venture fund as the restaurant chain looks to seed new ideas across agriculture, sustainability, supply chains and restaurant technology. The companies that received investments include: Benchmark Labs, founded by Carlos Gaitan, develops artificial intelligence (AI)-powered weather forecasting and climate intelligence technology by combining environmental data, machine learning and predictive analytics to help agriculture operators make more informed decisions, the company said. IMIO, founded by Charles Smith, said it develops microbial agricultural solutions designed to improve soil health, crop performance and farm productivity to reduce dependence on conventional chemical inputs.

articles.headline: Will Tim Hortons And Burger King Momentum Shift Restaurant Brands International's (QSR) Diversified Portfolio Narrative
articles.author: Sasha Jovanovic
articles.summary: Restaurant Brands International recently attracted fresh attention after Tim Hortons extended its comparable sales momentum and Burger King reported improved operating performance across its system. This combination points to healthier fundamentals in two of the company’s largest banners, reinforcing the benefits of its diversified quick-service portfolio. Next, we’ll examine how Tim Hortons’ sustained comparable sales momentum could influence Restaurant Brands International’s broader investment narrative and outlook. Invest in the nuclear renaissance through our list of 89 elite nuclear energy infrastructure plays powering the global AI revolution.

articles.headline: McDonald's Has A New Sauce Coming To Its Menu
articles.author: Mike Snider
articles.summary: McDonald's has a saucy menu option coming soon: Caesar sauce. The fast-food chain will begin offering McDonald's Caesar sauce on July 21 as a limited-time option on chicken menu items. The new Caesar sauce is described as "a creamy, garlicky parmesan blend infused with notes of lemon," according to McDonald's Menu Spotter site. Back in June, McDonald's began offering a FIFA World Cup 26 Meal with one of nine collectible cups featuring soccer stars such as current USMNT's Christian Pulisic, Spain's Lamine Yamal, Mexico's Santiago Gimenez, French superstar now Fox analyst Thierry Henry, LAFC forward Son Heung-min, David Beckham and others – including Grimace.

articles.headline: Pizzeria Buffet-Inspired QSR Menus
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: The Pizza Hut Throwback Value Menu is a new offering from the QSR brand that's inspired by its previous dining experiences to take aim at Millennial nostalgia in a cost-effective way. The menu encourages consumers to build their own Pizza Hut buffet experience by mixing and matching various items that can be conveniently ordered to their house. The menu is competitively priced as a nod to increasing calls for low-cost dining options with prices starting at $3 to encourage customers to order multiples of their favorites. The Pizza Hut Throwback Value Menu is being launched with the Back to the Hut digital experience to quiz consumers on their brand knowledge to unlock rewards and more. Both announcements come as part of the nostalgia marketing surge that's meant to capture the excitement consumers have about their childhood experiences with their favorite brands.

articles.headline: How Food Waste Tracking Can Unlock Stronger Restaurant Margins
articles.summary: For quick-service restaurant operators, performance is measured in seconds and cents. Operators track ticket times, labor ratios and food costs down to the unit. Yet one of the most consistent drivers of margin loss has received far less attention: surplus food from plates, preparation scraps, overpurchasing and more. That is beginning to change. As inflation, supply volatility and labor pressures continue to challenge profitability, operators are reexamining what gets thrown away and why. What they are finding is that these losses are not random. Surplus food is measurable, patterned and in many cases, preventable. In the United States, ReFED estimates that restaurants generate an estimated 11.4 million tons of food waste each year, representing roughly $25 billion in lost value. For QSR brands operating hundreds or thousands of units, even small reductions translate into meaningful gains.

articles.headline: Gusto! Introducing Adders And Snack To Menu
articles.summary: Atlanta-based fast-casual brand gusto! is introducing Adders and Snacks, two new menu offerings that give guests more choice and more occasions to enjoy the brand. Available beginning July 14 at all 13 metro Atlanta locations, the launch marks the first time guests can customize gusto!’s signature bowls and wraps, alongside a new lineup of snacks for today’s more flexible eating occasions. For the first time in gusto!’s 11-year history, guests can customize their favorite bowls and wraps with Adders—a collection of fresh ingredients that lets every guest put their own fresh twist on the flavors they love. At the same time, the Snacks menu introduces a new way to enjoy gusto!, with four menu items designed for everything from post-workout cravings and afternoon pick-me-ups to those moments when a full meal is more than you need.

articles.headline: Sauce-Focused Fried Chicken Menus
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: The McDonald's Caesar menu is a new series of items headed to the QSR brand's lineup for a limited time only to help satisfy customer interest in sauce-driven fried chicken items. The menu puts the brand's Caesar-style dipping sauce in the spotlight that boasts a creamy Parmesan blend base with notes of garlic and lemon throughout. The menu features the Bacon Caesar McCrispy, Caesar Snack Wrap and the McCrispy Strips with Caesar Sauce, which are each part of the brand's McCrispy platform to complement the new sauce. The McDonald's Caesar menu helps the QSR brand to catch the attention of fried chicken fans and also help to expand on its existing category prowess for a newstalgia twist that works well for foodies.

articles.headline: KFC Vs Chick-Fil-A: Which Serves A Healthier Classic Chicken Sandwich?
articles.author: Kashmir Brummel
articles.summary: If you're looking for a healthy option for lunch or dinner, a classic chicken sandwich probably won't be top of your list. But if you do have a craving that nothing else will satisfy, it's not necessarily off-limits; it might just be about making the best choice. On paper, KFC and Chick-fil-A have fairly similar offerings: a fried chicken filet and a couple of pickle slices on a bun, with KFC opting for mayo, while the Chick-fil-A bun is buttered. Despite this, there are some significant nutritional differences between the two. Before making your choice, you need to consider what makes food healthy or unhealthy? Generally speaking, healthy foods contain higher levels of beneficial nutrients such as protein and fiber and lower levels of saturated fat, sodium, or added sugar. Calorie count isn't necessarily an indicator of whether something is healthy or not, as long as you're not eating more calories than you need.

articles.headline: KFC Puts Iconic Cult-Classic Item Back On The Menu After Fans Demand Return: ‘Times Are Healing’
articles.author: Ben Cost
articles.summary: KFC is catering to nosh-talgic fast food fans by dusting off a cult classic dish from the late 20th century — popcorn chicken. On Monday, July 13, the red and white chicken chain started serving the crispy protein nuggets, marking a triumphant return for the fan-favorite food, per a press release. Originally debuting in the 1990s, Popcorn Chicken graced KFC menus until it was discontinued in 2023. By that point, this donut hole-ified fried chicken had earned a massive cult following, with the dish’s diehards flocking to social media to demand its return. So, by pop-ular demand, the chicken conglomerate decided to bring it back. “For years, KFC has heard directly from fans clamoring for Popcorn Chicken’s return,” said Melissa Cash, Chief Marketing Officer, KFC US. “We read every comment, every DM, and even every online petition signature, and the message was impossible to ignore.”

articles.headline: Health Officials Investigate Possible Taco Bell Lettuce Link To Cyclosporiasis Outbreak, Report Says
articles.author: Naina Srivastava
articles.summary: Public health officials are investigating whether lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants contributed to the outbreak of an intestinal infection, cyclosporiasis, the Washington Post reported Tuesday, citing unnamed sources. Federal officials said on Tuesday that infection patterns may differ by location, and that they believe cases in four states — Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky and West Virginia — are linked. Michigan has been hit hardest by the outbreak, tallying over 3,309 cases, according to the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services. Some who contracted the illness told investigators they had eaten at Taco Bell, a person familiar with the national investigation told the Post. But others who became sick had not eaten there. Taco Bell locations in the Detroit area posted notices that told customers they were “currently unable to sell Lettuce, Cilantro Onion, Pico de Gallo, and Guacamole due to a nationwide recall."

articles.headline: State Prosecutors Say Charges Altered In Food Adulteration Case At Upstate Wendy's
articles.author: Zach Rainey, Chloe McCoy
articles.summary: We are learning more about an alleged food adulterating case at an Upstate South Carolina Wendy's that resulted in the arrest of three employees. Back in June, three employees at the Wendy's in Union were charged with tampering with a food product following an incident that happened on May 30. In that incident, a customer reported to law enforcement that an employee advised her not to eat the food she had just purchased from the restaurant. According to the report, the employee told the customer that the food had been placed in a trash can, retrieved, spit on, and then rewrapped before being given to her. The solicitor's office said they completed their review of the incident on Wednesday, July 15, and concluded the most appropriate charge was adulteration of food, rather than food product tampering.

articles.headline: Fast Food Chain Continues Central Kentucky Expansion With New Restaurant Opening
articles.author: Brian Simms
articles.summary: A new-to-Richmond fast food chain is opening in the Central Kentucky town. Chipotle Mexican Grill is opening its first location in Richmond at 10:45 a.m. on Thursday, July 16. The new restaurant is located at 863 Eastern Bypass, near Hobby Lobby and Menards. The fast-casual restaurant chain specializes in bowls, tacos and burritos made to order in front of the customer. The new location is part of a massive expansion of Chipotle restaurants in recent years in Central Kentucky. New locations have opened or opening soon in Lexington, Georgetown, Frankfort and Nicholasville. The new Richmond location will feature a “Chipotlane,” a drive-thru pick-up lane that allows guests to pick up digital orders without leaving their cars. It will also have the restaurant’s new high protein menu with dishes that range from 15 to 81 grams of protein per item.

articles.headline: Chipotle’s Latest Expansion Plan Comes With Real Risk
articles.author: Tony Owusu
articles.summary: I remember the first time I brought my Mexican girlfriend to Chipotle (CMG). She had never heard of the fast food restaurant. Unlike Taco Bell, which seemingly has a mythical status in Mexico (though there are no locations there at the moment), Chipotle isn’t very popular south of the border. “Taco Bell? Chipotle blows Taco Bell out of the water,” I assured her and her family. But the truth was that I actually hadn’t eaten at Chipotle in years. If all goes well for Chipotle now, my girlfriend’s family won’t have to come to the U.S. to try the food. Chipotle announced that it will open its inaugural store in the Monterrey area on Thursday with plans to open other locations in the state of Nuevo Leon later in the year.

articles.headline: New Look For Burger King In Lufkin Promises A Fresh Dining Vibe
articles.author: Danny Merrell
articles.summary: The innovators at Burger King recently introduced its new 'Sizzle' concept, the company's latest attempt to freshen the brand and deliver a more appealing dining experience for its customers. Don't worry, their signature flame-grilled burgers are not on the chopping block. This is all part of the company's 'Reclaim the Flame' initiative, which was introduced in 2023. Since that time, numerous BK locations have been renovated to Sizzle standards, with many more upgrades on the way. This is much more than a coat of paint on a fading wall. According to a post on BurgerBeast.com, this complete redesign will incorporate "double-lane drive-thrus, digital kiosks, open-flame grills visible to customers, and interiors that feel closer to fast-casual than fast food." Some new menu items and options may also be a possibility.

articles.headline: Burger King Brings Back A Retro Chicken Favorite
articles.author: Moz Farooque
articles.summary: Fast-food menus continue to get louder as chains try to capture the attention of younger consumers whose noses are often buried in their phones. I still love the McDonald’s Big Mac because its appeal is straightforward: two beef patties, a familiar sauce, and a taste that has barely needed reinvention. Some of my younger friends, though, want more oomph. They gravitate toward oversized patties, hotter sauces, and increasingly aggressive flavor combinations. I’m not from the TikTok generation, so I don’t always “get it,” but the best fast food often comes down to a few recognizable ingredients prepared consistently. Burger King is leaning into that same idea, with a nostalgic twist. The chain is giving longtime fans a new reason to revisit it. Burger King is giving its long-running Original Chicken Sandwich two limited-time makeovers for summer, adding a Loaded Jalapeño version and a Club version at participating restaurants nationwide, according to BrandEating. The Loaded Jalapeño Original Chicken Sandwich pairs Burger King’s crispy, breaded white-meat chicken patty with creamy jalapeño sauce, crispy, pickled jalapeños, crispy onions, lettuce, tomatoes, and American cheese, all served on the chain’s familiar hoagie-style sesame-seed bun.

articles.headline: The Seriously Nostalgic Burger King Fries That Are Long Overdue For A Revival
articles.author: Rosie Govin
articles.summary: In a theoretical in-memoriam montage of discontinued Burger King items, one stands out as something that may have simply been too far ahead of its time to succeed when first released. Burger King Shake 'Em Up Fries were introduced in 2002 and consisted of French fries and a little baggie of cheese-flavored powder. You'd pour the cheese powder into the bag of fries, shake them up, and be left with cheesy, coated, flavorful spuds. In the early oughts, this proved to be a little too high-concept for Burger King customers, despite the iconic commercial. Shake 'Em Up Fries were discontinued within a year. But fast-forward a couple of decades, and fans are more than ready for their triumphant return. "Brooooo that was my childhood," one Redditor reminisced. "Go to Burger King for the Shake 'Em fries and the cream Icee. Head home and sit watching the Cartoon Network Friday Night line up."

articles.headline: The Next Generation Of QSR, Part 1: The New Economics Of Value
articles.author: Sasha Jovanovic
articles.summary: This is part one of a series. In Part 2 of The Next Generation of QSR series, we'll explore how loyalty programs, artificial intelligence and personalization are becoming the next frontier in creating value, strengthening guest relationships and driving long-term growth. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Consumer Price Index (CPI) for Food Away From Home (restaurants and other foodservice), the cost of dining out has increased by an estimated 30% over the past five years. That means quick-service brands are rethinking the term value. Specifically, what it means for their guests, their brands and their bottom lines.

articles.headline: FDA Outlines Risk-Based Future For Food Industry Oversight
articles.author: Virginia Lee
articles.summary: Food companies with strong compliance records could face fewer routine federal inspections as the FDA redirects resources toward higher-risk facilities, complex investigations and imported foods, as noted at this week’s IFT FIRST event. Dr. Donald Prater, the FDA’s acting deputy commissioner for human foods, outlined a changing regulatory model built around state partnerships, artificial intelligence and greater access to industry data. “This is not a reduction in oversight,” Prater said. “It’s a reallocation of FDA resources toward higher-risk, more complex work.” Prater said the FDA’s seeking new ways to address acute threats such as foodborne illness alongside chronic risks involving nutrition and food ingredients.

articles.headline: Fresh Kitchen Expands Leadership Team
articles.summary: Fresh Kitchen (FK), the healthy, fast, Modern-American bowl concept, has announced two additions to its leadership team: William “Bill” Knopf as SVP, Head of Operations and Matt Livingston as Chief Development Officer. Knopf joins Fresh Kitchen following senior operations roles at honeygrow and Noodles & Company. Livingston joins after more than a decade leading real estate, asset management and market development at First Watch Restaurants. The executives will lead two functions critical to Fresh Kitchen’s next phase of growth: operating incredible restaurants and developing the real estate pipeline required to support the company’s plan of 100+ remarkable restaurants in the coming years. “Bill and Matt have helped grow some of the most respected brands in the restaurant industry, but what matters most is that they embody the FK culture,” said Tyler Azzarelli, CEO of Fresh Kitchen. “They understand that growth starts with great people and remarkable restaurant execution. Bill will raise the bar on operations and leadership, while Matt will build the development pipeline for what comes next.”

articles.headline: How Casey’s Keeps Outperforming Its C-Store And QSR Peers
articles.author: Brett Dworski
articles.summary: In a presentation to a room full of analysts and investors in New York City in late June, Darren Rebelez laid out the framework for how Casey’s General Stores intends to grow over the next three years. The plan includes adding 400 convenience stores through a combination of new builds and small-scale acquisitions, growing inside sales by the mid-single digits and increasing EBITDA at an 8%-10% compound annual growth rate. Before attendees had a chance to ask how the company would achieve those goals, Rebelez, the retailer’s president and CEO, said simply: Casey’s will keep doing what Casey’s does.

articles.headline: Soccer-Celebrating QSR Menu Promotions
articles.author: Michael Hemsworth
articles.summary: McDonald's Saved Menu has been developed as a temporary transformation of its Saver Menu that's influenced by the World Cup to build on the excitement surrounding the sporting event. The promotional menu is inspired by England's goalkeeper Jordan Pickford and honors the player's achievement as the country's most-capped player with some exclusive deals for customers who visit restaurants in Washington to claim. The menu will be on offer on July 15, 2026 from 5pm to 6pm and will entitle customers to choose one item from the Saver Menu free of charge. The promotional menu will also be supported by the goalkeeper's signature penalty prediction cheat sheet in the form of a sticker on the brand's cold drink cups. The McDonald's Saved Menu promotion tackles the regional marketing surge that has been taking place on a global scale during the 2026 FIFA World Cup to accelerate foot traffic by targeting fan demand.

articles.headline: A Surprising Restaurant Chain Outgrew Starbucks, Chipotle, And Dunkin’ Last Year
articles.author: María José Gutiérrez Chávez
articles.summary: America’s fastest-growing restaurant isn’t Starbucks, Chick-fil-A, or Chipotle. It’s a chicken wing chain that’s been opening hundreds of new locations. In a preview of QSR Magazine’s annual QSR 50, which ranks the leading fast food chains, Wingstop led the 2026 cohort of restaurants. The full report is expected to be released in August. While in past years Starbucks has topped the ranking, this year Wingstop has earned the top spot by opening 382 new locations, around 100 more than any other brand. Wingstop has risen through the ranks, becoming the fourth-fastest-growing brand in 2024 and reaching the top spot in just under two years. At the time, Starbucks was ahead by 589 locations, but Wingstop has since caught up.

articles.headline: Krispy Kreme Turns 89, Rolls Out 89-Cent Dozen
articles.summary: Krispy Kreme will celebrate the brand's 89th birthday Friday, July 17, by offering 89-cent Original Glazed doughnuts by the dozen, the company announced in a news release. On Friday only, fans can score an Original Glazed dozen for just 89 cents with the purchase of any dozen at regular price. The promotion will run at participating U.S. locations. The limit is two dozen in-shop and at the drive-thru, while customers can purchase an extra one dozen for online pickup or delivery. "Every Krispy Kreme birthday is really a celebration of our fans," Alison Holder, Krispy Kreme chief brand and product officer, said in the press release. "Offering Original Glazed® dozens for just 89 cents is our way of saying thank you to the guests who make Krispy Kreme part of their celebrations, traditions and everyday sweet moments."

articles.headline: Chicken Finger Favorite Zaxbys Announces Further Expansion In Chicago Suburbs
articles.author: Rick West
articles.summary: Zaxbys, the popular fast-casual chicken chain from Georgia, announced its second Chicago-area development deal as it looks to expand its footprint in the Midwest. The seven-restaurant development deal comes six months after the brand opened its first Illinois location at 13429 Illinois Route 59 in Plainfield in January. “The response we've seen in Chicagoland has validated what we believed about the market from the beginning,” said Russ Holland, chief development officer at Zaxbys, in a news release. “Between the success of our Plainfield opening and the strong interest from experienced operators, we're building meaningful momentum in the region. This second development agreement is another important step in establishing Zaxbys as a long-term growth brand throughout Chicago and the broader Midwest.”
